title: Exercises: Laser Cooling
description: Practice calculations for Doppler and sideband cooling

exercises.title: Doppler Limit Calculation
exercises.description: Calculate the Doppler temperature limit for Ca+ and Yb+ ions, and convert to mean phonon number for a 1 MHz trap.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

# Constants
hbar = 1.055e-34
k_B = 1.38e-23

# Ca+ parameters
Gamma_Ca_MHz = 22  # Natural linewidth
omega_trap_MHz = 1.0

# Calculate Doppler temperature
Gamma_Ca = Gamma_Ca_MHz * 1e6 * 2 * np.pi
T_D_Ca = # Your calculation

# Calculate mean phonon number: n_bar = k_B*T / (hbar*omega)
omega_trap = omega_trap_MHz * 1e6 * 2 * np.pi
n_bar_Ca = # Your calculation

print(f"Ca+ Doppler limit: {T_D_Ca*1e3:.2f} mK")
print(f"Mean phonon number at Doppler limit: {n_bar_Ca:.0f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

hbar = 1.055e-34
k_B = 1.38e-23

# Ca+
Gamma_Ca = 22e6 * 2 * np.pi
T_D_Ca = hbar * Gamma_Ca / (2 * k_B)
omega_trap = 1e6 * 2 * np.pi
n_bar_Ca = k_B * T_D_Ca / (hbar * omega_trap)

print(f"Ca+ Doppler limit: {T_D_Ca*1e3:.2f} mK")
print(f"Mean phonon number: {n_bar_Ca:.0f}")
print(f"\nThis shows why sideband cooling is needed - Doppler cooling alone")
print(f"leaves ~{n_bar_Ca:.0f} phonons, far from the ground state!")

exercises.title: Sideband Cooling Analysis
exercises.description: Simulate the sideband cooling process and calculate the final ground state probability.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def sideband_cooling_simulation(n_initial: int, n_cycles: int, 
                                  eta: float, Gamma: float, omega: float) -> list:
    """
    Simulate sideband cooling.
    
    Args:
        n_initial: Initial phonon number
        n_cycles: Number of cooling cycles
        eta: Lamb-Dicke parameter
        Gamma: Spontaneous emission rate (MHz)
        omega: Trap frequency (MHz)
    
    Returns:
        List of mean phonon numbers after each cycle
    """
    pass

# Simulate cooling from n=10
n_history = sideband_cooling_simulation(n_initial=10, n_cycles=50, 
                                         eta=0.1, Gamma=1, omega=1)
print(f"Final n_bar: {n_history[-1]:.3f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def sideband_cooling_simulation(n_initial, n_cycles, eta, Gamma, omega):
    n_bar = n_initial
    history = [n_bar]
    
    # Cooling rate per cycle (simplified model)
    # Each RSB pulse removes ~1 phonon, with probability depending on n
    for _ in range(n_cycles):
        if n_bar > 0:
            # Probability to be in n>0 state and successfully cool
            cooling_prob = n_bar / (n_bar + 1) * (1 - (Gamma/(2*omega))**2)
            n_bar = n_bar * (1 - cooling_prob * 0.9)  # 90% efficiency
        history.append(n_bar)
    
    return history

n_history = sideband_cooling_simulation(10, 50, 0.1, 1, 1)
print(f"Initial n: 10")
print(f"Final n_bar: {n_history[-1]:.4f}")
print(f"Ground state probability: {1/(1+n_history[-1]):.3f}")
hints: Each RSB cycle removes approximately one phonon
